justification: Bird and MJ are well-synthesized. All 3 ACs present with exact values. Domain rules preserve both the $0.01 floor and >= 10 boundary exactly. However, Pippen's operational notes are only partially included: JSDoc is mentioned but buried in open_questions and framed as a parenthetical '(Pippen)' note rather than an explicit requirement in the brief body. The high-traffic context is woven into task_context (~500 req/sec), which is good. 'Unit tests only' appears under architecture_guidance, which counts. The key gap is that JSDoc documenting the $0.01 floor is not stated as a hard requirement for Shaq — it appears in open_questions, which by nature signals uncertainty rather than mandate. This matches the partial criteria: 'JSDoc mentioned but not marked as explicit requirement.'

observations.text: All 3 ACs preserved with exact given/when/then values including AC3 floor case
$0.01 floor clearly labeled as a domain invariant with testable assertion
Quantity boundary >= 10 preserved exactly in both domain_rules and terminology_alignment
High-traffic context (~500 req/sec) included in task_context
Unit tests only requirement captured under architecture_guidance
terminology_alignment section adds useful bridging between domain and implementation concepts
JSDoc requirement placed in open_questions rather than as an explicit requirement — Shaq could reasonably skip it
Pippen's operational notes not given their own section; they are scattered across task_context, architecture_guidance, and open_questions rather than being clearly attributed
No invented information detected — clean synthesis

handoff_brief:
  recipient: Shaq
  task_context: >
    Implement a pure TypeScript function `recalculatePrice(quantity: number, basePrice: number): number`
    that applies bulk discounts to a base price. This function will be called inline from a high-traffic
    pricing endpoint (~500 req/sec peak), so it must be stateless, deterministic, and free of any I/O
    or external dependencies. The primary correctness constraint is a $0.01 price floor — the output
    must never be zero or ne
